questions.question: An application has an RDS Multi-AZ deployment. What specifically happens during a failover, and how long does it typically take?
options: The standby begins serving read traffic immediately when primary CPU exceeds 80%.

When the primary instance fails, AWS automatically updates the database DNS endpoint to point to the synchronous standby instance in a different Availability Zone. The application reconnects to the same endpoint, now pointing to the new primary, typically within 60-120 seconds.
Multi-AZ failover requires a manual snapshot restore from S3, taking 20-30 minutes.
The standby takes over by cloning the primary instance on-demand, taking 5-10 minutes.
questions.answer: When the primary instance fails, AWS automatically updates the database DNS endpoint to point to the synchronous standby instance in a different Availability Zone. The application reconnects to the same endpoint, now pointing to the new primary, typically within 60-120 seconds.
questions.explanation: This is why applications must implement connection retry logic. The DNS CNAME that your app connects to stays the same — AWS transparently updates what it points to.

questions.question: In DynamoDB's single-table design, what architectural benefit justifies storing multiple entity types (Users, Orders, Products) in the same table?
options: Single-table design reduces DynamoDB storage costs by 50%.

DynamoDB cannot perform JOIN operations. Single-table design pre-joins related entities by collocating them under the same Partition Key, enabling a single query to retrieve an entity and all its related data in one round-trip with consistent single-digit millisecond latency.
Single-table design allows DynamoDB to support SQL queries.
It prevents DynamoDB from hitting its 400KB item size limit.
questions.answer: DynamoDB cannot perform JOIN operations. Single-table design pre-joins related entities by collocating them under the same Partition Key, enabling a single query to retrieve an entity and all its related data in one round-trip with consistent single-digit millisecond latency.
questions.explanation: In a relational DB: SELECT user + JOIN orders = 2 queries or a complex JOIN. In single-table DynamoDB: one KeyConditionExpression on PK=USER#123 returns both the user record and all their orders in one scan.

questions.question: What specific database performance problem does Amazon ElastiCache Redis solve when placed in front of Amazon RDS?
options: It encrypts RDS query results before they reach the application.

It absorbs repetitive read queries by storing frequently requested results in memory with sub-millisecond retrieval latency. Instead of every API request hitting RDS (5-20ms round trip), cache hits are served in microseconds, dramatically reducing RDS load and improving response times.
It prevents SQL injection attacks against the RDS database.
It automatically applies database indexes to RDS slow queries.
questions.answer: It absorbs repetitive read queries by storing frequently requested results in memory with sub-millisecond retrieval latency. Instead of every API request hitting RDS (5-20ms round trip), cache hits are served in microseconds, dramatically reducing RDS load and improving response times.
questions.explanation: A user profile page that was previously making 10 RDS queries on every page load can be cached in Redis for 5 minutes — reducing 10 database queries to 1 Redis GET for 99% of requests.
